name: Creole Spoon Bread
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large saucepan, heat 3 cups of milk until almost simmering.
2. Gradually stir in 1 cup of cornmeal (enriched self-rising).
3. Stir in 1 lb of cooked and finely chopped shrimp.
4. Add 1/3 cup of chopped green bell pepper, 1/3 cup of chopped parsley, and 1/3 cup of chopped onion.
5. Incorporate 2 tablespoons of vegetable shortening or oil and 1/4 teaspoon of hot pepper sauce.
6. Bring to a boil over medium heat; cook, stirring constantly, for an additional minute.
7. Remove from heat and let cool slightly.
8. Whisk the egg yolks into the cornmeal mixture.
9. Beat the egg whites until stiff but not dry; gently fold into the cornmeal mixture.
10. Pour the batter into a well-greased 2-quart baking dish.
11. Bake in a preheated oven at 375°F for 45 to 50 minutes, or until golden brown.
12. Meanwhile, prepare the sauce by melting butter in a saucepan and blending in 1/2 cup of flour (enriched self-rising).
13. Gradually stir in 1 cup of milk, cooking over medium heat while stirring constantly, until thickened.
14. Stir in 1 tablespoon of catsup and 1 tablespoon of Worcestershire sauce.
15. Serve the spoon bread with the sauce.
